prompt: I just ran a Python script that crashed. Stderr is in /tmp/eval-stderr.log. Show me the first 10 lines that mention 'error' or 'exception' (case-insensitive).
How many lines in /var/log/nginx/access.log are 5xx server errors? Just the count.
What's the single most common HTTP status code in the last 1000 lines of /var/log/nginx/access.log?
Find the IP address that hit the API the most times today in /var/log/nginx/access.log.
Show me requests that took longer than 1 second in /var/log/myapp/slow.log (request_time field is column 8, space-separated).
Tail the application log /var/log/myapp/app.log and only show me lines that contain 'ERROR' or 'WARN'.
I redirected my build output to /tmp/build.log. Show me the last 50 lines.
Count distinct user IDs that appeared in /var/log/myapp/access.log today (user_id is the last column).
How many .py files are in the current directory (not recursive — just top level)?
Find the 5 largest files in this repo and show their sizes.
List all log files in /tmp from today.
How many subdirectories are under /home/user/projects/? Just the count.
Show the file modification times for everything in ./scripts/ sorted newest first.
Find any file in this repo larger than 10MB.
Delete all .pyc files in this tree recursively, in a way that handles filenames with spaces.
Show me the disk usage of the top 10 largest subdirectories of /var/.
Find any occurrence of the literal string 'TODO' in this codebase under src/.
Where is the function 'main' defined under cmd/? Show file + line. Limit to first 3 matches.
Which test files in this repo mention the word 'integration' anywhere in their content?
Count occurrences of the literal string 'TODO' across the codebase.
Search this repo for any Go function whose name starts with 'New'. Show first 20 matches.
Find all .md files in this repo that contain a code block (look for triple-backticks).
Look for any deprecated API call patterns (regex: 'OldAPI.[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+') across the codebase, case-sensitive, with line numbers.
Is there a python3 process currently running on this machine?
Check whether ripgrep is installed on this host. Just tell me yes/no with the path if yes.
Show me which user owns each tmux session currently running on this host.
How many node processes are currently running?
Kill all running 'webpack-dev-server' processes.
Show me the 5 processes using the most CPU right now.
Does this user have docker available on their PATH?
Find the PID of the process listening on port 8080.
Run `go test ./...` and only show me the last 30 lines of output.
Build the Go project and capture stderr to /tmp/build.log. If it fails, show the last 20 lines of /tmp/build.log.
Show me lines 100 through 150 of cmd/weir/main.go.
Find all Go test files that currently fail when I run `go test ./...`. Just give me the package names.
Re-run the test suite every time any .go file in ./internal changes.
Time how long `make build` takes; compare it to `make build-fast`.
Show me the diff of changes between HEAD and origin/main for any .go file under internal/.
I have a file /tmp/users.json that's an array of {id, name, email} objects. Extract just the email field from each, one per line.
Pretty-print every .json file in the current directory tree.
Convert /tmp/data.csv (header in first row) to JSON-lines and write to /tmp/data.jsonl.
Pretty-print the CSV file /tmp/sales.csv as a table I can read in the terminal.
What's the mean of the 'response_time_ms' column in /tmp/metrics.csv? Just the number.
Fetch https://api.example.com/v1/status and extract the 'health' field from the JSON response.
Check whether https://example.com responds with a 200; just print YES or NO.
Which TCP ports are currently listening on this host?
Show me the most recent 5 commits to this repo with their full messages.
Which files were changed in the last 3 commits?
Find all the local branches that aren't yet merged into main.
What's the SHA-256 of every .tar.gz file in /tmp?
Replace every occurrence of 'oldname' with 'newname' across all .py files in this repo.
Hex-dump the first 256 bytes of /usr/bin/ls.
